mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market signals imminent recession risk, triggering risk-off positioning by institutional investors and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and retail sales indicates robust consumer demand and resilient household balance sheets, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.02 when both series rise concurrently, or forward returns are negative in the top quartile of joint growth observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production in a supply-constrained environment signals firm pricing power and expanding profit margins, which supports equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when INDPRO exceeds 102.5, indicating that output growth is being priced in or triggering stagflation fears.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating month-over-month consumer price inflation forces markets to price in prolonged hawkish Federal Reserve policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when inflation accelerates above the threshold, indicating markets have fully discounted the monetary response.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly expansion in real gross domestic product reflects broad-based economic growth, supporting corporate revenue trajectories and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative during consecutive quarters of GDP growth above 2.0% annualized, indicating decoupling between macro growth and equity earnings.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls alongside a stable or declining unemployment rate indicates healthy labor demand without wage-push inflation, supporting consumer spending and equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns decline when payroll growth exceeds 250k but unemployment falls below 3.5%, indicating wage-price spiral fears override employment gains.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts during a high interest rate environment signals severe credit tightening in the residential sector, dragging on financial sector profitability and consumer discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when housing starts fall by more than 10% quarter-over-quarter, indicating markets have already priced in the housing downturn.
